text.title: Stakeholder Register Structure
text.summary: Required fields for each register entry, the Power/Interest grid for prioritization, and the hidden-stakeholder checklist that prevents systematic omissions.

section.title: Register Fields
section.p: Each entry in the register must carry:

list.item: id — unique stable identifier (S-01, S-02, ...); never reuse after deprecation.
name — full name, validated against an authoritative directory (Entra ID, Workday, CRM); agents must not invent names.
role — position or function; source from directory, not assumption.
organization — department or external org.
category — one of: Sponsor, User, Customer, Team, Regulator, Supplier, Influencer.
power — H/M/L; documented with source evidence.
interest — H/M/L.
impact — H/M/L; how strongly the project affects them.
attitude — +/0/-/unknown; defaults to "unknown" without behavioral or quoted evidence.
quadrant — derived from power/interest: Manage Closely / Keep Satisfied / Keep Informed / Monitor.
cadence — communication frequency; required for all High-power stakeholders.
owner — PM or team member responsible for this relationship.
evidence — list of quotes, transcript references, or log entries supporting attitude and power assertions.
last_engaged — date of most recent substantive interaction.
hidden_flag — true if discovered via the hidden-stakeholder checklist, not initial analysis.

rule.statement: Store contact information via a secrets manager (e.g., 1Password op:// URL), never inline in the register; registers contain PII and are shared more broadly than contact credentials should be.
rule.rationale: Inline contact data in a shared repo becomes a PII leak vector; referencing by key allows the register to be shared while keeping personal details protected.

section.title: Power/Interest Grid
section.p: Plot each stakeholder on the Mendelow 2x2 grid to determine engagement intensity:

list.item: Manage Closely (High power, High interest) — actively engage, involve in decisions, weekly contact minimum.
Keep Satisfied (High power, Low interest) — regular updates with no surprises; engage before decisions that affect them.
Keep Informed (Low power, High interest) — regular communication, solicit feedback; these are your champions-in-waiting.
Monitor (Low power, Low interest) — minimal effort; watch for changes in attitude or power.

rule.statement: For programs with regulatory or political risk, overlay Mitchell-Agle-Wood salience (power x legitimacy x urgency); the 2x2 alone flattens political complexity that drives high-stakes blockers.
rule.rationale: Influence is not the same as seniority; gatekeepers (EAs, infosec PMs, procurement leads) often hold de facto veto power that the 2x2 maps to Low-power because they lack formal title.

rule.statement: Run the hidden-stakeholder checklist at project initiation and at each phase gate; systematic omissions are more common than random ones.
rule.rationale: The same categories are missed on most projects: legal, infosec, procurement, FinOps, accessibility, works council/union, downstream API consumers, regulators. A checklist converts "we forgot" into "we confirmed absent."

list.item: Legal / Compliance — data processing agreements, IP, contracts.
Information Security — access, encryption, audit trail.
Procurement / FinOps — vendor contracts, cloud spend approval.
Accessibility — a11y requirements for regulated or public-facing products.
Works Council / Union — organizational change requiring co-determination.
Downstream API / Data Consumers — systems or teams that depend on outputs.
Regulators / Standards Bodies — external bodies with compliance authority.
Customer Success / Support — teams absorbing the operational change.
